questions.q: What is the main idea behind the theory of evolution through natural selection?
questions.answer: The main idea behind the theory of evolution through natural selection is that individuals with favorable variations are better adapted to their environment and therefore more likely to survive and reproduce, passing on their advantageous traits to their offspring.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of adaptive radiation? (a) Darwin's finches (b) Peppered moths (c) Antibiotic resistance in bacteria (d) All of the above
Fill in the blank: The process of evolution is based on the concept of _______ which states that all living organisms share a common ancestry.
Describe the role of genetic drift in the process of evolution.
questions.answer: Genetic drift is the random change in the frequency of a gene or genetic trait in a population over time. It can occur due to various factors such as genetic mutations, gene flow, or random sampling errors. Genetic drift can lead to the loss or fixation of a particular gene or trait, and can therefore contribute to the process of evolution.

questions.q: What is the difference between homologous and analogous structures? Give one example of each.
questions.answer: Homologous structures are those that have a common origin but may not have a similar function, whereas analogous structures have a similar function but do not have a common origin. Example of homologous structure: forelimbs of vertebrates, example of analogous structure: wings of birds and insects.

questions.q: True or False: The theory of evolution is only based on fossil evidence.
Explain the concept of convergent evolution with the help of an example.
questions.answer: Convergent evolution is the process by which different species, lineages, or organisms evolve similar traits or characteristics in response to similar environmental pressures or selection forces. Example: the evolution of wings in birds and insects, which are structurally different but serve the same function of flight.

questions.q: Which of the following is a direct evidence of evolution? (a) Fossil record (b) Comparative anatomy (c) Molecular biology (d) All of the above
Fill in the blank: The term _______ refers to the development of new species from an existing species.
Describe the role of gene flow in the process of evolution.
questions.answer: Gene flow is the movement of genes from one population to another, which can lead to the exchange of genetic information and increase genetic diversity. It can also lead to the loss of unique genetic traits and reduce the genetic differences between populations.
